{{Quote|You have to admire the Chiss. They don't just steal your technology—they make it better.|Anonymous Imperial engineer|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels" />}}
The '''''Nssis''-class Clawcraft''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] typically associated with the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]], but in fact was a hybrid development of standard [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] technology.

==Characteristics==
[[File:Clawcraft_negvv.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] Clawcraft [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]

The fuselage was based on the ubiquitous "ball cockpit" of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] of Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], with a [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar]] [[Ion engine/Legends|ion drive]] pod mounted on the rear, but stemming from the junction of cockpit and drive pod were two pairs of curving wings which thrust out like an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing's]] [[S-foils/Legends|strike-foils]], and then extended forward in a claw-like grip around the cockpit. At the tip of each wing was a Sienar [[L-s7.2 laser cannon/Legends|L-s7.2 laser cannon]].

With modest deflector [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and thicker hull armor, Clawcraft were slower at [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speeds than their [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] progenitors, although the ion engines allowed the ship to achieve an atmospheric speed of 1,000 kilometers per hour, and a respectable sublight acceleration of almost forty thousand meters per second squared.

[[File:Clawcraft_Chiss.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Chiss Clawcraft]]

Clawcraft made up for their lower sublight speed by greater maneuverability. Each curved wing could make minor position adjustments and was paired with a mounted control jet to allow a Clawcraft's pilot to throw the ship into sharp turns and complex maneuvers.

A unique [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] beacon [[Chiss anchor-point network|system]] allowed a Clawcraft to make lightspeed jumps without an on-board [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]], enabling it to function as a long-range craft while significantly reducing the weight, size and complexity of non-combat equipment carried.

==History==
[[Clawcraft (pre-Empire)|Clawcraft fighters]] had been produced for some time before the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] under the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] began sharing technology with the Chiss Ascendancy, an action initiated by the Chiss Imperial [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. Models of Clawcraft began incorporating [[TIE starfighter|TIE]] design cues as part of the joint venture.<ref name="TUR" />

[[File:Chiss_Clawcraft_SotG.jpg|thumb|left|180px|[[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] Clawcraft in an asteroid field.]]

By [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], pilots from the Household Phalanx were using long-range [[Scout ship/Legends|scout vessels]] combining TIE cockpits and drives with alien technology in a manner similar to these Chiss-Imperial Clawcraft.

The ''Nssis''-class was revealed to the wider galaxy in [[25 ABY]], when the [[force Spike]], a group of [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] pilots from the Household Phalanx led by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jagged Fel]] that consisted of three squadrons, including [[Spike Squadron (Empire of the Hand)|Spike Squadron]], fought at [[Battle of Garqi|Garqi]] and [[Battle of Ithor|Ithor]]. A part of this force continued to fight alongside [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] until at least the battle of [[Battle of Kalarba|Kalarba]], and after the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

[[File:Clawcraft.jpg|thumb|right|180px|A [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] Clawcraft.]]

The Chiss force returned to their home territory until Colonel Jagged Fel convinced Baron Fel to allow him to take a scout force to further determine the danger posed by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. This led to Colonel Fel and [[Shawnkyr Nuruodo]] to deploy in a a wing-pair of Household Phalanx Clawcraft where they participated in [[Battle of Hapes (Yuuzhan Vong War)|operations]] in the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]]. Colonel Fel's group were later joined by several squadrons of combat pilots that formed [[Vanguard Squadron (Hapes)|Vanguard Squadron]] at the time. The two Clawcraft also fought alongside the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] with [[Twin Suns Squadron]] until the end of the war.

A few ''Nssis''-class ships were seen in Chiss space in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], by which time political developments in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] had led to Baron Fel becoming [[Assistant Syndic]] of the CEDF; but it was not until the clashes with the [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] Colony in [[36 ABY]] and [[38 ABY]] that the familiar Clawcraft categorically appeared in large numbers in CEDF forces.
